response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished polyglot engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but his profile fundamentally mismatches this role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He has no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience (RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech, not capital markets systems), and no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle work. His depth is in web/full-stack TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, which does not transfer to this consulting engagement's core needs.
strengths: Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and testing expertise
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Broad polyglot background with some C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, wave field synthesis thesis) and fluent English
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience (only MySQL/MongoDB), and no Summit platform exposure

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and CI/CD fundamentals
